title: Lattice energy and Born-Haber cycles
summary: 9701 A Level thermochemistry — lattice energy, Born–Haber cycles, and Fajan's rules with PhET energy simulation.
learningObjectives: Define lattice energy and state its sign.
Explain factors affecting lattice energy magnitude.
Relate discrepancies to covalent character in ionic compounds.

simpleExplanation.title: Energy of ionic lattice formation
simpleExplanation.summary: Lattice energy is the exothermic energy change when gaseous ions form a solid ionic lattice. Born–Haber cycles apply Hess's law to find it indirectly.
steps: ΔH_latt: M⁺(g) + X⁻(g) → MX(s) — always exothermic.
Cycle: ΔH_f° = Σ(atomisation + IE + EA + sublimation) + ΔH_latt.
Higher charge and smaller ions → more exothermic ΔH_latt.
Experimental vs theoretical gap → partial covalency (Fajan’s rules).

sections.content: **Born–Haber cycles** (23.1) are high-mark A Level questions — every arrow must be **labelled** with the correct enthalpy change. Link back to **Hess's law** (5.2).
